location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

seniority.level: Staff / Group PM (people manager, IC6+ equivalent)
level_signals: 6+ years of experience in product management
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Group PM title implies managing a team of PMs
Comp range $176K-$220K base consistent with senior/staff PM at Lyft

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in product management, product design, or equivalent consulting experience with significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps or marketplace products
2+ years of experience managing product managers
significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps or marketplace products
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation
Natural ability to make things happen around you — you manage project ambiguity, complexity, and interdependencies in an organized and structured way
Strong ability to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products and the health of the business
Excellent judgment and thoughtfulness about the dynamics of a product ecosystem
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level — within teams, with partner teams, and to executive leadership
Ability to conceptualize, manage, and prioritize multiple projects for stakeholders
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills — balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working with or alongside data science and operations teams on complex, products; familiarity with experimentation, marketplace dynamics, or logistics is a plus

scope.surface: Airports, scheduled rides, and events verticals on the Lyft platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Rider and driver experience quality (trust, predictability)
Booking conversion / growth in vertical segments
Marketplace efficiency (rider-driver matching in high-variability environments)
core_responsibilities: Manage and grow a team of product managers, providing development, mentorship, and coaching to help them achieve their goals and Lyft's goals
Set an ambitious vision for the Verticals team that motivates your team, peers, and leadership — and that accounts for the distinct dynamics of airports, scheduled rides, and events

Understand Lyft's strategic and competitive position. Set your team's goals and success metrics that align with Lyft's mission and drive maximum impact based on data analysis, market research, and usability studies
Completely own your team's products — existing and new — from ideation through development, experimentation, launch, growth, and maintenance, caring deeply about both rider and driver experiences
Lead and align cross-functional partners including engineers, designers, data scientists, analysts, marketers, business partners, and operations to drive a shared vision
Communicate clear roadmaps, priorities, experiments, and decisions across a wide spectrum of audiences from partner teams to executive leadership

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) with equity mentioned
Clear product surface with three distinct verticals named (airports, scheduled rides, events)
Explicit people-management scope (managing a team of PMs) — senior growth opportunity
Hybrid schedule with specific days named — predictable structure
Strong benefits package (18 weeks parental leave, 401k match, medical/dental/vision)
High visibility role described as having 'outsized impact on Lyft's core business'
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear whether candidates requiring sponsorship are eligible
In-office requirement is strict: 3 specific days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in San Francisco only — eliminates most remote candidates
Salary range is wide ($44K spread) and described as dependent on location and qualifications, making offer predictability low
Role spans three operationally complex verticals simultaneously — scope may be very demanding

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years required, comp range, and people management scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and product areas; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The JD requires '6+ years of experience in product management, product design, or equivalent consulting experience' — it is unclear whether consulting-only candidates without direct PM experience would be accepted or screened out. Treated as hard given unconditional bullet phrasing.

The nice-to-have around 'data science and operations teams' experience mentions 'familiarity with experimentation, marketplace dynamics, or logistics is a plus' — parsed as soft/nice-to-have based on explicit 'is a plus' qualifier.
